Ars-s-f..personality: The Sulphur type is the 'ragged philosopher' — a disheveled, sedentary intellectual absorbed in abstract speculation and invention, indifferent to personal appearance, cleanliness, and social convention. He is self-absorbed, ungrateful, and lives in disorder, caring little for the opinions or needs of others while believing himself superior to educated society.
Ars-s-f..mental_state: The mind is marked by philosophical mania — an obsessive, fruitless tracing of causes to their origins, dwelling on unanswerable abstract questions without productive outcome. There is confusion of thought, inability to concentrate, aversion to systematic work, and a dullness upon waking that makes gathering oneself after sleep difficult. The patient may oscillate between inventive fixation and complete mental indolence.
Ars-s-f..emotional_pattern: The core emotional pattern is one of deep selfishness combined with religious melancholy and despair — the patient may sink into moaning prayer, convinced he has sinned beyond redemption, or may become proudly delusional, imagining himself in possession of beautiful things while surrounded by rags. There is a fundamental dissatisfaction and satiety of life, longing for death, too lazy to rouse himself yet too unhappy to live, with startling nervousness and restless, dream-disturbed sleep.
